text:
To determine how many yellow balloons Benny threw bologna at, we can set up an equation based on the information provided.

Given:
- Benny throws 2 pieces of bologna at each red balloon.
- Benny throws 3 pieces of bologna at each yellow balloon.
- Benny threw 58 pieces of bologna in total.
- Twenty of the balloons are red.

First, we calculate the number of pieces of bologna thrown at the red balloons:
\[ 2 \text{ pieces} \times 20 \text{ red balloons} = 40 \text{ pieces} \]

This means the remaining pieces of bologna were thrown at the yellow balloons:
\[ 58 \text{ total pieces} - 40 \text{ pieces for red balloons} = 18 \text{ pieces for yellow balloons} \]

Since each yellow balloon receives 3 pieces of bologna:
\[ \text{Number of yellow balloons} = \frac{18 \text{ pieces}}{3 \text{ pieces per yellow balloon}} = 6 \]

Therefore, the number of yellow balloons is:
\[ \boxed{6} \]
